friction_items.description: 344 tools (464 registered entries; 197 unique handlers) exposed to MCP. A researcher saying 'help me do DE on this GEO accession' triggers a router but the listing surface is enormous — many tools share prefixes (tool_audit_*, tool_grounding_*, tool_synthesis_*, tool_synthesize, tool_synthesize_plan, tool_synthesis_curate_figures, tool_synthesis_preview) without clear hierarchy. The advertised UX is 'just talk to me' but the underlying surface punishes any AI client without semantic routing.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Consolidate near-duplicates (already partly done for tool_ground unifying tool_grounding_register + tool_ground_from_context). Continue with tool_audit_figure / tool_audit_figure_full / tool_audit_figure_coverage / tool_audit_figure_interactivity into one tool with a `scope` arg. Same for tool_synthesize / tool_synthesize_plan / tool_synthesis_preview.

friction_items.description: Scenario is 'RNA-seq DE with R Bioconductor + Python scanpy + GEO accession'. Zero protocols are RNA-seq-specific. wet_lab pack covers bench prep (SOP, plate maps, reagents, instrument run logs) — not bioinformatics analysis. There is no DESeq2 / edgeR / limma protocol, no scanpy protocol, no GEO accession ingest protocol, no QC-by-FastQC protocol, no alignment-vs-pseudoalignment guidance, no batch-effect / surrogate-variable protocol. A domain researcher would hit wet_lab/method/wet_lab_experiment_design and find it talks about animals, mice, blinding — not their downloaded counts matrix.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Either (a) add a bio_omics pack with RNA-seq / scRNA-seq protocols, or (b) make this explicit in routing — when 'GEO accession' or 'DESeq2' or 'scanpy' is in the prompt, route to methodology/external_tool_setup + methodology/mixed_language_orchestration + literature/literature_search and warn the researcher that domain-specific scaffolds aren't present.

friction_items.description: wet_lab_experiment_design.yaml description block reads like a research methods textbook ('the most expensive error in wet-lab science', 'three biological replicates without randomization across plate position confounds the effect of interest with the well's edge-evaporation gradient'). A computational researcher arriving here for RNA-seq help would skim it as 'this is for bench people' and bounce. The editorial voice is right for the bench audience but actively wrong-shaped for the dry-lab one.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a methodology/bioinformatics_pipeline_design.yaml protocol scoped to dry-lab DE/scRNA-seq design, OR split wet_lab_experiment_design into wet_lab_bench_design + bioinformatics_analysis_design.

friction_items.description: tool_audit_quality_full description is one 600-character paragraph naming 6 child tools, two it does NOT run (with conditional 'NB:' caveats), a grounding-blocker prefix string, and three downstream tools that DO run the per-step literature gate. A researcher (or even an AI agent) seeing 'blockers found' from this tool has to read three other tool descriptions to find out which gate fired. Error messages from this composite gate will inherit the same density.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Return structured blocker categories ({completeness:[], code:[], prose:[], claims:[], prereg_diff:[], grounding:[]}) and trim the prose description to 1-2 sentences naming the categories. Move the 'this audit does NOT run literature' caveat into a dedicated `lit_gate_status` field returned by the tool.

friction_items.description: First 5 turns for a researcher pasting 'here is my GEO accession GSE12345, help me do DE' would be: (1) sys_boot, (2) sys_protocol_get(project_startup) — text-heavy, (3) tool_intake_autofill — needs files dropped into inputs/, (4) tool_route(prompt) — likely lands on methodology/external_tool_setup OR literature/literature_search since no RNA-seq protocol exists, (5) tool_research_method or tool_research_tool to discover DESeq2/scanpy. That's 4-5 turns of pure scaffolding before any DE-relevant action. Researcher patience is brittle.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a tool_quick_start equivalent for common omics queries that skips the intake gate when only a single accession + a one-liner intent is supplied. Or a tool_route fast-path for keyword 'GEO|SRA|GSE|GSM' that pre-bundles literature_search + external_tool_setup + a stub DE protocol.

friction_items.description: Scenario asks for end-to-end DE workflow ending in synthesis. The shipped path produces synthesis/{paper,poster,dashboard,abstract,...} from workspace/<step>/conclusions.md. But there is no protocol that explicitly walks DE-output volcano-plot + heatmap + GSEA-result-table → conclusions.md. The researcher has to glue tool_synthesis_curate_figures + custom code. Likely outcome: workflow exits at 'execute' tier with no synthesized paper because grounding-verify blocks on un-cited methodological decisions (DESeq2 vs edgeR, prefilter thresholds, batch correction).
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a synthesis/methods_section_bioinformatics.yaml mirroring the existing methods_section_wet_lab.yaml. Pre-seed grounding records for the common DE method choices (DESeq2 Love 2014, edgeR Robinson 2010, limma-voom Law 2014) so a researcher picking from the canonical menu doesn't get a grounding blocker on every method line.

friction_items.description: Scenario explicitly mentions R Bioconductor + Python scanpy. methodology/mixed_language_orchestration.yaml exists, but tool_route is keyword/embeddings-driven — a researcher who never says 'mixed language' won't land here. They'll set up Python, run scanpy, then hit a wall when the methodology says 'use limma-voom' (R) and there's no scaffolding for the handoff.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add explicit triggers ['DESeq2', 'edgeR', 'limma', 'scanpy', 'Seurat', 'Bioconductor', 'reticulate', 'rpy2'] to mixed_language_orchestration router entry. Or fold its content into external_tool_setup so the R+Python concern surfaces at setup-time.

friction_items.description: Methodology folder mixes generic statistical protocols (bayesian_analysis, bootstrapping_design, multiple_comparisons), domain-flavoured ones (cox_ph_diagnostics, clinical_trials, survey_psychometrics, qualitative_research, qualitative_pii_redaction, qualitative_quality_audit), and meta-protocols (methodology_selection, methodological_consultation, pick_tool_stack, tool_discovery). A researcher told 'pick a methodology' from the protocol list (43 entries in methodology/) faces a wall.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Subdivide methodology/ into stats/, study_design/, computational/, qualitative/ subfolders. Or add tier='plan' and tags to every protocol so tool_route surfaces by subdomain.

friction_items.description: tool_audit_quality_full does NOT run per-step literature gate but tool_audit_synthesis DOES. tool_path_finalize ALSO runs it. A researcher who runs the master audit, fixes blockers, then hits synthesize, gets a fresh batch of literature-gate blockers they couldn't see earlier. The description warns about this but a researcher won't read the description.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Either include per-step literature gate in tool_audit_quality_full by default (with skip arg for power users), or surface a 'lit_gate_pending=true' flag on the master audit output so the user knows another gate is coming.

friction_items.description: project_startup.check_intake_freshness returns recommended_depth ∈ {full, refresh-only, skip}. A researcher dropping a fresh GEO accession into an existing project will hit 'refresh-only' (intake substantive, <90d) and the AI is told 'do NOT regenerate'. But the new data IS substantively different. The freshness gate is data-blind — it only knows about file age, not file novelty.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a hash-of-inputs check alongside the timestamp. If inputs/raw_data has a hash not seen in intake.md's input_hashes block, force 'full' regardless of age.

friction_items.description: tool_route, tool_semantic_route, tool_quick_route, sys_semantic_tool_search all overlap: route a prompt → something. Researcher confusion compounds when the AI client calls one and gets nothing useful, then tries another.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Mark tool_semantic_route and sys_semantic_tool_search as DEBUG/POWER-USER tier in their short descriptions. Default-list only tool_route + tool_quick_route to MCP listing.

friction_items.description: _optional_dep_inventory + sys_dep_inventory exist, but the failure mode on a missing dep is RuntimeError raised at call site. A researcher whose AI client tries tool_latex_compile without LaTeX installed gets a raw stack trace instead of 'install with brew install mactex'.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Wrap _MissingDependency.__call__ to return _error('Install with: pip install ...; for system-level deps, see external_tool_setup protocol') instead of raising. Already partially there — finish the pattern.

recommendations: Ship a methodology/rnaseq_de.yaml (and methodology/scrnaseq_qc.yaml) protocol authored for the dry-lab audience — DESeq2/edgeR/limma-voom decision tree, GEO/SRA ingest with checksum verification, pre-filter + size-factor + batch-correction defaults, common-pitfall list (low-count filtering before vs after normalization, 'using counts as input to limma without voom', etc).
Add fast-path tool_route triggers for 'GEO', 'GSE', 'SRA', 'fastq', 'DESeq2', 'scanpy', 'Seurat', 'limma', 'edgeR', 'AnnData' so the router doesn't dump the researcher into wet_lab_experiment_design.
Consolidate the 7 audit_figure_* tools into one tool_audit_figure with mode={'basic','coverage','interactivity','full'}. Same for tool_synthesize family.

Add bench-vs-dry-lab disambiguation to wet_lab pack detector so a project with .fastq + counts.csv + an .Rmd does NOT trigger wet_lab routing (currently the file-ext heuristic only catches instrument-native formats).
Trim default MCP list_tools surface to ~80 'frontline' tools; gate the rest behind sys_tool_describe / sys_semantic_tool_search. 344 tools is too many for any model context window to rank fairly.

Pre-seed grounding records for canonical bioinformatics method citations (DESeq2/Love2014, edgeR/Robinson2010, limma-voom/Law2014, scanpy/Wolf2018, Seurat/Hao2023) so researchers don't hit grounding-verify blockers on every off-the-shelf choice.

Make tool_audit_quality_full include the per-step literature gate by default, with skip=['literature'] for power users — surface 'lit_gate_pending=true' if skipped so the researcher isn't ambushed at synthesis time.
Replace _MissingDependency RuntimeError raises with structured _error responses pointing at external_tool_setup.
